title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum swelling
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum swelling are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, created by soaking plant material in a mixture of water and alcohol. These tinctures offer a natural and effective way to reduce gum swelling. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with gum swelling include goldenseal, myrrh, and echinacea. Goldenseal has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe irritated gums. Myrrh is known for its antiseptic qualities, aiding in the healing of gum tissue. Echinacea boosts the immune system, which can help the body fight off gum infections causing swelling. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can experience relief from gum swelling, leading to improved oral health, less pain, and an overall enhancement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to help reduce pain and promote healing in the gums. Additionally, yarrow tincture can stimulate blood flow to the gums, which can help to speed up the healing process and alleviate discomfort. Its astringent properties can also help to tighten and tone gum tissue, reducing the risk of further infection and inflammation. Overall, yarrow tincture can provide natural relief for gum swelling and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation in the affected area, thanks to compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Its astringent properties tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing swelling and bleeding, due to tannins present in yarrow.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum infections, thus alleviating swelling, attributable to volatile oils and alkaloids.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of yarrow tincture help relieve pain associated with gum swelling by numbing the affected area, owing to the presence of lactones and alkaloids.

Wound healing: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing, which aids in the faster recovery of swollen gums, as it contains compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and tannins that stimulate tissue regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants.
Coarsely chop the herbs and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely submerged, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herbs. Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, the date, and the dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a soothing film when it comes into contact with moisture. This film can help to protect and relieve irritated gum tissue, reducing inflammation and swelling. Additionally, marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can further help to promote gum health and prevent infection. Its ability to stimulate the production of collagen and speed up the healing process also makes it an effective remedy for gum swelling. Overall, marshmallow tincture is a natural and gentle way to soothe and heal irritated gums.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tincture creates a protective film on the mucous membranes, providing relief from gum swelling due to its soothing effect, thanks to the high mucilage content.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce gum swelling by minimizing inflammation, owing to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing gum swelling caused by infections, attributed to the tannins and polysaccharides.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of marshmallow tincture helps alleviate gum swelling-related pain by numbing the affected area, due to the presence of triterpene saponins.
Wound healing: Marshmallow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, reducing gum swelling and facilitating recovery, owing to the mucilage and flavonoid content.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried marshmallow root into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture on low heat for 30 minutes, then let it cool for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Add 1-2 drops of peppermint oil (optional for flavor and additional anti-inflammatory properties) and mix well.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months, shaking before each use. Use 1-2 mL (1/4-1/2 teaspoon) up to 3 times a day as needed for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area. Arnica has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including oral health issues. The tincture works by stimulating the flow of blood to the gums, which helps to promote healing and reduce swelling. Additionally, arnica has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infection in the gums. When used regularly, arnica flower tincture can help to maintain healthy gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and helenalin.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain associated with gum swelling due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Arnica flower tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections, containing active constituents like essential oils and phenolic acids.
Circulatory stimulant: It improves blood flow to the gums, promoting healing and reducing swelling, due to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.
Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture neutralizes free radicals that can worsen gum swelling, with active constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh arnica flowers, carefully avoiding the toxic roots and stems.
Clean the flowers gently, then let them wilt for several hours to reduce their moisture content.
Chop the wilted arnica flowers and place them in a clean glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters capacity.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the chopped flowers until the jar is completely full, then seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days, before straining and storing the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums. The herb's active compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, have been shown to have a soothing effect on irritated tissues, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with gum swelling. Additionally, wild indigo has natural antimicrobial properties that can help fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of gum infections that can cause swelling. By using wild indigo tincture as a natural remedy, individuals can find relief from gum swelling and support their oral health in a safe and effective way.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild indigo tincture can help reduce gum swelling by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, thanks to its active constituents, alkaloids and polyphenols.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture helps decrease inflammation and swelling in gums by limiting the production of inflammatory chemicals, due to the presence of active compounds like indigo and indirubin.
Analgesic properties: Wild indigo tincture can help alleviate pain associated with gum swelling, as it contains compounds like indican and baptisin that can numb the affected area.

Immunostimulant properties: The tincture can boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections causing gum swelling, as it contains immune-enhancing compounds like alkaloids and polysaccharides.
Astringent properties: Wild indigo tincture can help tighten gum tissues and reduce excess secretions, thanks to its tannins content, which aids in reducing gum swelling and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest wild indigo (Baptisia tinctoria) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides or pollutants.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of the wild indigo plant material, filling a clean, dry jar, leaving 1 inch of space at the top.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the plant material, completely covering it, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, gently shaking it every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material. Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from direct sunlight. For gum swelling, use 1-2 mL of the tincture up to three times a day. Consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and usage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can stimulate the production of collagen, which is essential for maintaining healthy gum tissue. Its astringent properties can also help tighten and tone gum tissue, reducing the risk of further inflammation and infection. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a natural and effective remedy for treating gum swelling and promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for gum swelling as it helps to alleviate pain and promote healing. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms, which is crucial for gum swelling as it helps to prevent infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include the alkaloids and saponins.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes wound healing, which is important for gum swelling as it helps to speed up the recovery process. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and polysaccharides.

Analgesic: The tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for gum swelling as it helps to alleviate discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include the triterpenes and volatile oils.

Astringent: Pot marigold tincture has astringent properties, which is important for gum swelling as it helps to tighten and strengthen tissues. This property is due to the presence of tannins and resins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, gently rinse, and let dry for 12 hours.
Chop dried flowers into small pieces, fill a clean jar, and add 80-proof vodka until flowers are fully submerged.
Seal the jar, label with date and contents, and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Administer 1-2 mL of the pot marigold tincture 3 times a day for gum swelling, adjusting dosage as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and improve oral health. The tincture is made by extracting the bioactive compounds from white tea leaves in a solvent, usually alcohol or glycerin, which makes the beneficial compounds more concentrated and easily absorbed by the body. White tea is rich in polyphenols, particularly catechins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and plaque in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum disease and inflammation. By using white tea tincture as a mouthwash or applying it directly to the affected area, it can help soothe and heal swollen gums, promoting better oral hygiene and overall dental health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea tincture's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum swelling by decreasing the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, thanks to its high concentration of polyphenols and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum inflammation, due to the presence of catechins and tannins.

Antioxidant: White tea tincture's antioxidant property helps protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, supporting overall oral health, owing to the rich presence of epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechins.

Analgesic: The analgesic property of white tea tincture helps alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with swelling, by inhibiting pain-signaling molecules, attributed to its active constituents like gallic acid and theaflavins.

Anti-collagenase: White tea tincture's anti-collagenase property promotes gum health by preventing the breakdown of collagen, which maintains gum tissue structure, due to the presence of catechins and other polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried white tea leaves and 2 cups of healing herbs (like chamomile or calendula) into small pieces.
Combine herbs and tea leaves with 2 cups of vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use as needed for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and help alleviate pain. The herb is rich in triterpenoid saponins, which are known to strengthen the connective tissues in the gums, promoting healing and reducing swelling. Additionally, gotu kola has been shown to improve circulation, which can help increase the supply of oxygen and nutrients to the gums, further promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Regular use of gotu kola tincture can help maintain healthy gums and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture reduces gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents such as triterpenoid saponins (asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid).
Wound healing: Gotu kola promotes gum tissue regeneration and quickens the healing process of inflamed gums, due to the presence of triterpenes and asiatic acid.
Antimicrobial: Gotu kola fights gum swelling by inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections, as a result of its active compounds like tannins and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola tincture helps to reduce gum swelling by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative damage, which is facilitated by its rich antioxidant content, including triterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Circulation improvement: Gotu kola enhances blood flow, ensuring proper nutrient supply to the gums and supporting gum health, due to the active constituents like triterpenes (asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid) and saponins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried gotu kola leaves and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 5 ounces of vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely covered.
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid from the jar using a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent herbs.
Store the final tincture in a sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use 1-2 mL up to three times a day for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The active compounds in chamomile, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. Additionally, chamomile has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infections that may contribute to gum swelling. By using a chamomile tincture as a mouthwash or applying it directly to the affected area, it can help to soothe and heal irritated gums, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, such as alpha-bisabolol and chamazulene.
Antimicrobial: Its ability to kill or inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth contributes to reducing gum swelling, due to the presence of volatile oils like azulene and bisabolol.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture alleviates gum swelling-related pain by numbing the affected area, attributable to its active compounds, including matricin and apigenin.
Wound healing: The tincture promotes faster healing of inflamed gums, as it stimulates tissue regeneration and reduces inflammation, owing to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils.

Vascular dilation: German chamomile tincture improves blood flow to the gums by promoting vasodilation, which aids in the healing process and helps reduce swelling, due to its active constituents, such as spiroether and farnesene.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the chopped flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from heat and light. Use 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains high levels of echinacea, a powerful antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compound. This compound works by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation, which in turn helps to alleviate gum swelling. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to promote tissue regeneration, which can help to repair damaged gum tissue. The tincture form of eastern purple coneflower also allows for quick absorption into the bloodstream, providing fast-acting relief for gum swelling. Overall, the anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties of echinacea make eastern purple coneflower tincture an effective natural remedy for gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tincture reduces gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the active constituents such as chrysosplenol D and luteolin.
Antimicrobial: Its ability to kill or inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth helps alleviate gum swelling, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The tincture helps manage gum swelling-related pain by numbing the affected area, owing to the active constituents like cichoric acid and eupafolin.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tincture promotes gum tissue healing and reduces swelling by neutralizing free radicals, attributable to its high antioxidant content, including cichoric acid and chlorogenic acid.
Immune-boosting: The tincture strengthens the immune system, enabling the body to fight infections causing gum swelling, due to the active constituents like polysaccharides and alkamides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and dry, then rinse and air dry.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried herb into small pieces, place in a clean glass jar, and add 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum.
Tightly seal the jar, shake well, and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out all liquid, and store in a cool, dark place.
Administer 30-60 drops of the tincture up to three times a day for gum swelling, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry tincture helps with gum swelling because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gums. The tincture is made from the leaves of the teaberry plant, which is rich in tannins and essential oils that have astringent properties. These astringents can help tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the chance of further irritation. Additionally, teaberry tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum swelling and other oral health issues. By using teaberry tincture regularly, individuals may be able to alleviate gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Teaberry tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for gum swelling as it helps to alleviate pain and promote healing. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins and volatile oils in teaberry.

Antibacterial: Teaberry tincture inhibits bacterial growth, which is crucial for gum swelling as it helps to prevent infection and further irritation. This is due to the presence of compounds such as ursolic acid and gallic acid.

Astringent: Teaberry tincture tightens and strengthens tissues, which is beneficial for gum swelling as it promotes tissue repair and reduces bleeding. This is attributed to the tannins present in teaberry.

Analgesic: Teaberry tincture alleviates pain, which is important for gum swelling as it provides relief and promotes oral health. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as eugenol and methyl salicylate.

Antioxidant: Teaberry tincture neutralizes free radicals, which is significant for gum swelling as it supports overall oral health and helps to reduce inflammation. This is attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh Teaberry (Gaultheria procumbens) leaves, rinse, and finely chop.
Combine chopped leaves with 1 quart of 100-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring leaves are completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, discarding the spent leaves.

Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Adults may use 1-2 mL up to three times daily as needed for gum swelling. Consult a healthcare provider for appropriate dosage for children.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum swelling with herbal tinctures is by using a tincture of echinacea, goldenseal, or myrrh. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce swelling and fight infection. Dilute the tincture in water and use it as a mouthwash, holding it in your mouth for several minutes before spitting it out. Repeat this process several times a day. Additionally, a tincture of calendula or chamomile can be used as a soothing rinse to help reduce redness and irritation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures include inflammation of the mucous membranes, mouth ulcers, and tissue irritations. Herbs like echinacea, goldenseal, and myrrh are often used in tincture form to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing. These natural remedies can provide relief from discomfort and help speed up the recovery process. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
